comments.body:
This is awesome, thanks for the blog post. I'm keen to try it out 😁  I have one question... _Are you still able to flick the light switch manually, using your finger, or does the servo prevent you from doing so?_

I recently started experimenting with some DIY projects using a Wemos board (similar to the NodeMCU), and my first project was automating my garage door. I hooked it up to my Google Assistant using IFTTT and also built my own web app in Angular and .Net to control it. My next project might just be this one of yours 😁
comments.body_html:
This is awesome, thanks for the blog post. I'm keen to try it out 😁 I have one question... Are you still able to flick the light switch manually, using your finger, or does the servo prevent you from doing so?

I recently started experimenting with some DIY projects using a Wemos board (similar to the NodeMCU), and my first project was automating my garage door. I hooked it up to my Google Assistant using IFTTT and also built my own web app in Angular and .Net to control it. My next project might just be this one of yours 😁

comments.body:
With the plate I designed originally you could sort of manually manipulate the servo out of the way to flip the switch, but it wasn't what I would call completely accessible! 

I had plans to make a version where the servo could be rotated out of the way, but in the end (and after an office re-shuffle) I ended up uninstalling the automation from the light switch...

Maybe if I get bored in the future I'll revisit this!
comments.body_html:
With the plate I designed originally you could sort of manually manipulate the servo out of the way to flip the switch, but it wasn't what I would call completely accessible!

I had plans to make a version where the servo could be rotated out of the way, but in the end (and after an office re-shuffle) I ended up uninstalling the automation from the light switch...

Maybe if I get bored in the future I'll revisit this!

comments.body:
Hello,

i have prepared my raspi and Game HAT as described in the blog. I have installed retropi, added the configuration for the arcade_joysick_rpi, copied the Pico-8, install the wiringpi und modify the retropiscript. It works fine but i am not possible to use the joystick and the buttons in Pico-8. So my question:
Is it necessary to install the mk_arcade_joystick_rpi? The retropi simulation works fine.
How is the correct key configuration to use the buttons and the joystick? What i have to do?

It would be fine to get some hints.

Your Volker
comments.body_html:
Hello,

i have prepared my raspi and Game HAT as described in the blog. I have installed retropi, added the configuration for the arcade_joysick_rpi, copied the Pico-8, install the wiringpi und modify the retropiscript. It works fine but i am not possible to use the joystick and the buttons in Pico-8. So my question:
Is it necessary to install the mk_arcade_joystick_rpi? The retropi simulation works fine.
How is the correct key configuration to use the buttons and the joystick? What i have to do?

It would be fine to get some hints.

Your Volker

comments.body:
Hi there, unfortunately I'm using my Pi in another project now so can't confirm the settings.

You could try setting up the Game HAT manually as per the instructions here: https://www.waveshare.com/w/upload/2/22/Game_HAT_user_manual_en.pdf

and then follow my instructions for installing pico8 and making the changes in `/etc/profile.d/10-retropie.sh`

Hope that helps!
comments.body_html:
Hi there, unfortunately I'm using my Pi in another project now so can't confirm the settings.

You could try setting up the Game HAT manually as per the instructions here: https://www.waveshare.com/w/upload/2/22/Game_HAT_user_manual_en.pdf

and then follow my instructions for installing pico8 and making the changes in /etc/profile.d/10-retropie.sh

Hope that helps!

comments.body:
Sad to see it go. I was not an active member recently, but the boards always were a great resource to check for any updates on china handhelds.
Is there a discord or similar available?
comments.body_html:

comments.body:
@Lukas-Sander I had created a Discord for Dingoonity, but I didn't push it very much as I was opposed to encouraging posting content to a closed/non-indexable system such as Discord.

I believe many are using the [Retro Handhelds Discord](https://discord.com/invite/RetroHandhelds) and similar communities.

now all we have left is gbatemp

comments.body: After years of being in the GP2X community I moved over to Dingoonity when I got my A320, and later my GCW Zero.  I'm glad to see the site archived but am sad to see it not active.  I wonder if all of my themes for Dingux and Gmenu survive...
